ask: We should show why a job got flagged as ghost.

story.title: Ghost Flag Explanation – Show Why a Job Got Flagged as Ghost
story.story: As a job seeker reviewing flagged listings, I want to see the rationale and deterministic signals behind a job's ghost classification, so that I can understand the evidence supporting the verdict and decide how much weight to give it.

acs.name: Rationale Sentence Displayed Alongside Ghost Score
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page viewing a job classified as GHOST
acs.when: the job seeker views the ghost score section for that job
thens: The one-sentence rationale generated by the ghost-score classifier will be displayed adjacent to the ghost score.
The rationale will be visually associated with the ghost score label (e.g., within the same evidence panel component).
The rationale will be framed as supporting evidence, not as a definitive certainty, per GUARDRAILS.md requirements.

acs.name: Three Deterministic Signals Displayed in Evidence Panel
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page viewing a job classified as GHOST with all three signals computable
acs.when: the job seeker views the evidence panel for that job
thens: The repost frequency over 90 days signal will be displayed with its computed value.
The posting age signal will be displayed with its computed value.
The cross-board spread signal will be displayed with its computed value.
All three signals will be presented as evidence supporting the model's verdict, not as certainties.

acs.name: Evidence Panel Shown for REAL-Classified Jobs
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page viewing a job classified as REAL
acs.when: the job seeker views the ghost score section for that job
thens: The one-sentence rationale will be displayed alongside the REAL verdict.
The three deterministic signals will be displayed with their computed values.
The evidence panel will frame the signals as supporting evidence, not as certainties.

acs.name: Stale Data Disclaimer for Repost Frequency Signal
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page viewing a job's evidence panel
acs.when: the job seeker views the repost frequency signal
thens: A disclosure will be displayed indicating that repost frequency data may be up to 24 hours old.
The repost frequency value shown will reflect the most recent daily batch ingest, not real-time data.

acs.name: Null or Missing Rationale Graceful Degradation
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page viewing a job whose classifier failed to generate a rationale string
acs.when: the job seeker views the ghost score section for that job
thens: The rationale sentence will not be displayed.
A fallback message (exact copy to be confirmed) will be shown in place of the rationale.
The three deterministic signals will still be displayed if available.
No unhandled error or blank space will be rendered in the rationale area.

acs.name: Partial Signal Display When One or More Signals Are Uncomputable
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page viewing a job that lacks sufficient history to compute one or more deterministic signals
acs.when: the job seeker views the evidence panel for that job
thens: Signals that were successfully computed will be displayed with their values.
Signals that could not be computed will display a placeholder value (exact label — e.g., "N/A" or "Not available" — to be confirmed per A2).
The evidence panel will not throw an error or collapse due to missing signal data.

acs.name: Zero Cross-Board Spread Displays Without Error
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page viewing a job that was posted on a single board only
acs.when: the job seeker views the cross-board spread signal in the evidence panel
thens: The cross-board spread signal will display "0" or "Not detected" (exact label to be confirmed).
No error state or blank value will be rendered for the cross-board spread field.

acs.name: Unauthorized Access to Evidence Panel
acs.given: an unauthenticated visitor attempts to access the Analyze page for a ghost-flagged job
acs.when: the visitor navigates to the Analyze page URL directly
thens: The evidence panel and ghost score rationale will not be rendered.
The visitor will be redirected to the authentication flow or shown an access-denied state.

acs.name: Signal Data Fetch Failure
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page and the data layer fails to return signal data for a job
acs.when: the job seeker views the evidence panel for that job
thens: The evidence panel will display an error state indicating that signal data could not be loaded.
The ghost score and verdict label will still be displayed if available.
No unhandled exception or blank panel will be rendered.

assumptions_carried: A1 — The one-sentence rationale is always generated by the classifier and is non-null; if it can be null, error handling must be specified separately
A2 — The three deterministic signals are always computable for any job; if a job lacks sufficient history to compute a signal, the behavior (hide, show "N/A", show "0") is not yet defined
A3 — The rationale and signals are stored in or joinable to the job record in the data layer; no new data pipeline or ETL is required
A4 — "Analyze page" refers to a specific existing UI surface; if multiple pages show ghost scores, scope is limited to one page unless clarified
A5 — The 24-hour latency on repost detection is acceptable for this feature; no real-time or sub-daily refresh is required
A6 — Users should not be able to dispute or provide feedback on individual ghost verdicts as part of this feature (feedback mechanism is out of scope unless stated)

researcher_brief:
# CONTEXT BRIEF: "Show why a job got flagged as ghost"

- **Ghost-score classification already exists with rationale.** The classifier returns GHOST or REAL plus a one-sentence rationale, and three deterministic signals are computed (repost frequency over 90 days, posting age, cross-board spread). Currently only the score is shown on the Analyze page, not the rationale or signals. ([C2] Ghost-score classification)

- **Evidence panel is a documented design requirement.** GUARDRAILS.md explicitly
analyst_spec:
# SCOPED SPEC: Show Why a Job Got Flagged as Ghost

## IN SCOPE:
- Display the one-sentence rationale generated by the ghost-score classifier on the Analyze page
- Surface the three deterministic signals (repost frequency over 90 days, posting age, cross-board spread) that contributed to the ghost classification
- Present the rationale and signals alongside the existing ghost score
- Frame the explanation as evidence supporting the model's verdict, not as certainty (per guardrails requirement)
-

clarity.reasoning: The generated story has a clear persona, single-action intent, and plain language. The title is slightly verbose but acceptable. The story sentence is slightly compound ('understand the evidence' and 'decide how much weight') but mirrors the reference's intent closely. Minor verbosity compared to the reference's cleaner phrasing.

testability.reasoning: All ACs follow GIVEN/WHEN/THEN structure with observable outcomes. Edge cases are thoroughly covered (missing rationale, partial signals, zero spread, auth failure, data fetch failure). Exceeds the reference in edge case coverage while maintaining testable, future-tense outcomes throughout.

scope_fidelity.reasoning: The generated story introduces several elements not present in the reference: a stale data disclaimer for repost frequency (AC4), unauthorized access handling (ACE4), signal data fetch failure (ACE5), and specific named signals (repost frequency, posting age, cross-board spread). While some additions are reasonable, the stale data disclaimer and auth flow are clearly invented scope beyond the original ask. The reference keeps scope tighter.

completeness.reasoning: The generated story surfaces all reference ACs and assumptions, then adds substantial additional coverage. It explicitly handles the REAL verdict case (AC3, matching A1 from reference), raw signal values (matching A2), missing signals (ACE2, matching ACE1 in reference), and adds multiple additional edge cases. Assumptions are well-flagged with A1-A6 prefixes. Slightly over-specified but very complete.

grounding.reasoning: Most claims are tied to context with assumptions flagged using A1-A6 prefixes. The reference to GUARDRAILS.md in AC1 suggests grounding in actual project documentation. However, AC4's stale data disclaimer and specific signal names (repost frequency, posting age, cross-board spread) appear as concrete claims without explicit PLACEHOLDER markers, suggesting some ungrounded specificity. Overall grounding discipline is good but not perfect.
